org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.sink

## Class TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction<IN,TXN,CONTEXT>

  * [java.lang.Object](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Object.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang")
  *     * [org.apache.flink.api.common.functions.AbstractRichFunction](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/AbstractRichFunction.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.common.functions")
    *       * [org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.sink.RichSinkFunction](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/sink/RichSinkFunction.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.sink")<IN>
      *         * org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.sink.TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction<IN,TXN,CONTEXT>

  * Type Parameters:
    `IN` \- Input type for [`SinkFunction`](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/sink/SinkFunction.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.sink").
    `TXN` \- Transaction to store all of the information required to handle a transaction.
    `CONTEXT` \- Context that will be shared across all invocations for the given [`TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction`](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/sink/TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.sink") instance. Context is created once

All Implemented Interfaces:
    [Serializable](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/io/Serializable.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.io"), [Function](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/Function.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.functions"), [RichFunction](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/RichFunction.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.functions"), [CheckpointListener](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/state/CheckpointListener.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.runtime.state"), [CheckpointedFunction](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/checkpoint/CheckpointedFunction.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.checkpoint"), [SinkFunction](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/sink/SinkFunction.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.sink")<IN>

Direct Known Subclasses:
    [FlinkKafkaProducer011](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/connectors/kafka/FlinkKafkaProducer011.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.connectors.kafka")

* * *




        [@PublicEvolving](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/annotation/PublicEvolving.html> "annotation in org.apache.flink.annotation")
        public abstract class TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction<IN,TXN,CONTEXT>
        extends [RichSinkFunction](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/sink/RichSinkFunction.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.sink")<IN>
        implements [CheckpointedFunction](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/checkpoint/CheckpointedFunction.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.checkpoint"), [CheckpointListener](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/state/CheckpointListener.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.runtime.state")

This is a recommended base class for all of the [`SinkFunction`](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/sink/SinkFunction.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.sink") that intend to implement exactly-once semantic. It does that by implementing two phase commit algorithm on top of the [`CheckpointedFunction`](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/checkpoint/CheckpointedFunction.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.checkpoint") and [`CheckpointListener`](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/state/CheckpointListener.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.runtime.state"). User should provide custom `TXN` (transaction handle) and implement abstract methods handling this transaction handle.

See Also:
    [Serialized Form](<../../../../../../../serialized-form.html#org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.sink.TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction>)

      * #### TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction

                                              [TypeSerializer](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/typeutils/TypeSerializer.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.common.typeutils")<[CONTEXT](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/sink/TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction.html> "type parameter in TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction")> contextSerializer)

Use default [`ListStateDescriptor`](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/state/ListStateDescriptor.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.common.state") for internal state serialization. Helpful utilities for using this constructor are [`TypeInformation.of(Class)`](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/typeinfo/TypeInformation.html#of-java.lang.Class->), [`TypeHint`](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/typeinfo/TypeHint.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.common.typeinfo") and [`TypeInformation.of(TypeHint)`](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/typeinfo/TypeInformation.html#of-org.apache.flink.api.common.typeinfo.TypeHint->). Example:


             TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction(TypeInformation.of(new TypeHint<State<TXN, CONTEXT>>() {}));

                                             throws [Exception](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Exception.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang")

Method that starts a new transaction.

Returns:
    newly created transaction.
Throws:
    `[Exception](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Exception.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang")`
      * #### preCommit

            protected abstract void preCommit([TXN](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/sink/TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction.html> "type parameter in TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction") transaction)
                                       throws [Exception](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Exception.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang")

Pre commit previously created transaction. Pre commit must make all of the necessary steps to prepare the transaction for a commit that might happen in the future. After this point the transaction might still be aborted, but underlying implementation must ensure that commit calls on already pre committed transactions will always succeed.

Usually implementation involves flushing the data.

Throws:
    `[Exception](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Exception.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang")`
      * #### commit

            protected abstract void commit([TXN](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/sink/TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction.html> "type parameter in TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction") transaction)

Commit a pre-committed transaction. If this method fail, Flink application will be restarted and [`recoverAndCommit(Object)`](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/sink/TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction.html#recoverAndCommit-TXN->) will be called again for the same transaction.

      * #### recoverAndCommit

            protected void recoverAndCommit([TXN](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/sink/TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction.html> "type parameter in TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction") transaction)

Invoked on recovered transactions after a failure. User implementation must ensure that this call will eventually succeed. If it fails, Flink application will be restarted and it will be invoked again. If it does not succeed eventually, a data loss will occur. Transactions will be recovered in an order in which they were created.

      * #### abort

            protected abstract void abort([TXN](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/sink/TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction.html> "type parameter in TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction") transaction)

Abort a transaction.

      * #### recoverAndAbort

            protected void recoverAndAbort([TXN](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/sink/TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction.html> "type parameter in TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction") transaction)

Abort a transaction that was rejected by a coordinator after a failure.

                                     [SinkFunction.Context](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/sink/SinkFunction.Context.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.sink") context)
                              throws [Exception](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Exception.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang")

Description copied from interface: `[SinkFunction](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/sink/SinkFunction.html#invoke-IN-org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.sink.SinkFunction.Context->)`

Writes the given value to the sink. This function is called for every record.

You have to override this method when implementing a `SinkFunction`, this is a `default` method for backward compatibility with the old-style method only.

Specified by:
    `[invoke](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/sink/SinkFunction.html#invoke-IN-org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.sink.SinkFunction.Context->)` in interface `[SinkFunction](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/sink/SinkFunction.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.sink")<[IN](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/sink/TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction.html> "type parameter in TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction")>`
Parameters:
    `value` \- The input record.
    `context` \- Additional context about the input record.
Throws:
    `[Exception](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Exception.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang")` \- This method may throw exceptions. Throwing an exception will cause the operation to fail and may trigger recovery.
      * #### notifyCheckpointComplete

            public final void notifyCheckpointComplete(long checkpointId)
                                                throws [Exception](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Exception.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang")

Description copied from interface: `[CheckpointListener](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/state/CheckpointListener.html#notifyCheckpointComplete-long->)`

This method is called as a notification once a distributed checkpoint has been completed. Note that any exception during this method will not cause the checkpoint to fail any more.

Specified by:
    `[notifyCheckpointComplete](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/state/CheckpointListener.html#notifyCheckpointComplete-long->)` in interface `[CheckpointListener](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/state/CheckpointListener.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.runtime.state")`
Parameters:
    `checkpointId` \- The ID of the checkpoint that has been completed.
Throws:
    `[Exception](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Exception.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang")`
      * #### snapshotState

            public void snapshotState([FunctionSnapshotContext](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/state/FunctionSnapshotContext.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.runtime.state") context)
                               throws [Exception](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Exception.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang")

Description copied from interface: `[CheckpointedFunction](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/checkpoint/CheckpointedFunction.html#snapshotState-org.apache.flink.runtime.state.FunctionSnapshotContext->)`

This method is called when a snapshot for a checkpoint is requested. This acts as a hook to the function to ensure that all state is exposed by means previously offered through [`FunctionInitializationContext`](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/state/FunctionInitializationContext.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.runtime.state") when the Function was initialized, or offered now by [`FunctionSnapshotContext`](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/runtime/state/FunctionSnapshotContext.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.runtime.state") itself.

Specified by:
    `[snapshotState](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/checkpoint/CheckpointedFunction.html#snapshotState-org.apache.flink.runtime.state.FunctionSnapshotContext->)` in interface `[CheckpointedFunction](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/checkpoint/CheckpointedFunction.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.checkpoint")`
Parameters:
    `context` \- the context for drawing a snapshot of the operator
Throws:
    `[Exception](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Exception.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang")`

      * #### close

            public void close()
                       throws [Exception](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Exception.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang")

Description copied from interface: `[RichFunction](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/RichFunction.html#close-->)`

Tear-down method for the user code. It is called after the last call to the main working methods (e.g. _map_ or _join_). For functions that are part of an iteration, this method will be invoked after each iteration superstep.

This method can be used for clean up work.

Specified by:
    `[close](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/RichFunction.html#close-->)` in interface `[RichFunction](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/RichFunction.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.api.common.functions")`
Overrides:
    `[close](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/AbstractRichFunction.html#close-->)` in class `[AbstractRichFunction](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/api/common/functions/AbstractRichFunction.html> "class in org.apache.flink.api.common.functions")`
Throws:
    `[Exception](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/Exception.html?is-external=true> "class or interface in java.lang")` \- Implementations may forward exceptions, which are caught by the runtime. When the runtime catches an exception, it aborts the task and lets the fail-over logic decide whether to retry the task execution.
      * #### setTransactionTimeout

            protected [TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/sink/TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.sink")<[IN](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/sink/TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction.html> "type parameter in TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction"),[TXN](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/sink/TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction.html> "type parameter in TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction"),[CONTEXT](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/sink/TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction.html> "type parameter in TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction")> setTransactionTimeout(long transactionTimeout)

Sets the transaction timeout. Setting only the transaction timeout has no effect in itself.

Parameters:
    `transactionTimeout` \- The transaction timeout in ms.
See Also:
    [`ignoreFailuresAfterTransactionTimeout()`](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/sink/TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction.html#ignoreFailuresAfterTransactionTimeout-->), [`enableTransactionTimeoutWarnings(double)`](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/sink/TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction.html#enableTransactionTimeoutWarnings-double->)
      * #### ignoreFailuresAfterTransactionTimeout

            protected [TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/sink/TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.sink")<[IN](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/sink/TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction.html> "type parameter in TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction"),[TXN](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/sink/TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction.html> "type parameter in TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction"),[CONTEXT](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/sink/TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction.html> "type parameter in TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction")> ignoreFailuresAfterTransactionTimeout()

If called, the sink will only log but not propagate exceptions thrown in [`recoverAndCommit(Object)`](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/sink/TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction.html#recoverAndCommit-TXN->) if the transaction is older than a specified transaction timeout. The start time of an transaction is determined by [`System.currentTimeMillis()`](<http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/lang/System.html?is-external=true#currentTimeMillis--> "class or interface in java.lang"). By default, failures are propagated.

      * #### enableTransactionTimeoutWarnings

            protected [TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/sink/TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.sink")<[IN](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/sink/TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction.html> "type parameter in TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction"),[TXN](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/sink/TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction.html> "type parameter in TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction"),[CONTEXT](<../../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/api/functions/sink/TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction.html> "type parameter in TwoPhaseCommitSinkFunction")> enableTransactionTimeoutWarnings(double warningRatio)

Enables logging of warnings if a transaction's elapsed time reaches a specified ratio of the `transactionTimeout`. If `warningRatio` is 0, a warning will be always logged when committing the transaction.

Parameters:
    `warningRatio` \- A value in the range [0,1].
Returns:
